caption: This video clip shows a presentation slide for a course titled "CRYOGENIC ENGINEERING." The slides are labeled "Tutorial – 4" and focus on "Liquid yield." Each slide features a mathematical formula for "y" presented in a blue box, which calculates liquid yield using variables such as $h_1, h_2, \varepsilon, h_f,$ and $h_g$. The NPTEL logo appears in the bottom-left corner, and a footer credits "Prof. M D Atrey, Department of Mechanical Engineering, IIT Bombay." As the sequence progresses, the content on the slide evolves. Initially, only the formula is visible. Later, the value "$\varepsilon = 0.90$" is added to the right of the equation. Finally, a data table appears below the formula, listing values for pressure "p (bar)", temperature "T (K)", enthalpy "h (J/g)", and entropy "s (J/gK)" across four columns labeled "1", "2", "f", and "g".
